📊 Visual Cheatsheet

The whole syllabus,
in one scrollable poster.

Every key concept across the 8 modules — distilled into diagrams, color-coded panels, octagons, body charts and flow arrows. Built for fast recall on exam day.

01
IKS Corpus & Vedic Foundation
Vedas · Vedāṅgas · Oral tradition · Yajñas
📜The Four Vedas
Each Veda has a designated priest and core function.
🔥
Ṛgveda
Hotṛ
Hymns & invocations. The oldest and largest — 10,552 mantras.
🪔
Yajurveda
Adhvaryu
Procedures for performing yajñas. Has Śukla and Kṛṣṇa branches.
🎵
Sāmaveda
Udgātṛ
Mantras set to melodic chants — origin of Indian music.
🌿
Atharvaveda
Brahma
Everyday life, medicine, healing, household rituals.
🌳Inside Each Veda — Four Layers
Every Veda has these four progressive layers, from ritual to realisation.
Saṃhitā — core mantras in metrical form
↓
Brāhmaṇa — technical SOPs for rituals (60–70% of the corpus)
↓
Āraṇyaka — philosophical interpretations (forest texts)
↓
Upaniṣad — Ātma Vidyā · knowledge of self (108 preserved)
⚙️The Six Vedāṅgas — Limbs of the Veda
Six auxiliary disciplines that preserve and decode the Vedas.
Kalpa branches: Śrauta (Vedic rituals) · Śulba (rope-geometry for altars) · Gṛhya (household) · Dharma (social duties)
🪷Pañcamahāyajñas — The Five Daily Sacrifices
📖
Brahma
Yajña
Reciting and teaching scripture
☀️
Deva
Yajña
Honouring natural forces — sun, fire, rain
🙏
Pitṛ
Yajña
Honouring ancestors
🤝
Manuṣya
Yajña
Helping the poor and guests
🐦
Bhūta
Yajña
Feeding animals and insects
🎼Vedic Meters (Chandas)
Gāyatrī

3 × 8 = 24

Three pādas of eight syllables each

Anuṣṭup

4 × 8 = 32

Four pādas of eight syllables each

🔑Foundational Concepts
Apauruṣeya
Non-human origin — Vedas were revealed to ṛṣis, not authored
Śruti
"That which is heard" — preserved through oral transmission
Ṛta
The cosmic order and rhythm underlying all existence
14 Vidyasthānas
4 Vedas + 6 Vedāṅgas + Purāṇas + Nyāya + Mīmāṃsā + Dharma Śāstra
Oral preservation
Ślokas, sūtras and mnemonics — built for brevity and memorisation
64 Kalās
Non-literary practical arts and skills
02
Philosophical Systems
Darśanas · Āstika · Nāstika · Tri-guṇa
🔍What Every Darśana Investigates
Darśana = "to see". Every system inquires into three entities — and all aim at Mokṣa.
🧘
Jīva
The individual self / soul
🌍
Jagat
The external world / matter
✨
Īśvara
The supreme being
🌳The Great Divide — Āstika vs Nāstika
Criterion is not belief in God — it is whether the school accepts the authority of the Vedas.
Āstika (Orthodox) — Accept the Vedas
  • Sāṅkhya · Kapila · Jagat · Puruṣa vs Prakṛti
  • Yoga · Patañjali · Aṣṭāṅga Yoga · mind control
  • Nyāya · Gautama · logic and epistemology
  • Vaiśeṣika · Kaṇāda · atomic theory · 7 Padārthas
  • Pūrva Mīmāṃsā · Jaimini · Vedic rituals
  • Vedānta · Bādarāyaṇa · Brahman · Upaniṣads
Nāstika (Heterodox) — Reject the Vedas
  • Cārvāka · materialism · only direct perception is valid
  • Jainism · Anekāntavāda · Jīva vs Ajīva · Triratna
  • Buddhism · Four Noble Truths · Eightfold Path · momentariness
Three shared beliefs across most schools: Karma (every action has consequence) · Saṃsāra (cycle of rebirth) · Free will (despite past karma)
👯The Six Schools — Three Traditional Pairs
Sāṅkhya — Yoga
Sāṅkhya analyses the world (Jagat) into 24 elements. Yoga applies this knowledge through Aṣṭāṅga discipline.
Nyāya — Vaiśeṣika
Nyāya handles logic and inference. Vaiśeṣika handles metaphysics and atomic theory (Paramāṇu).
Mīmāṃsā — Vedānta
Pūrva Mīmāṃsā focuses on ritual action. Uttara Mīmāṃsā (Vedānta) focuses on self-knowledge.
🎨Tri-guṇa — Three Qualities of Prakṛti
Borrowed from Sāṅkhya. Like primary colours, they combine to explain all behavioural diversity.
Sattva
Light · Illuminating
Clarity, knowledge, balance, peace
Rajas
Active · Restless
Ambition, desire, passion, drive
Tamas
Heavy · Concealing
Lethargy, ignorance, inertia
🪷Vedānta — Three Sub-schools
Advaita
Śaṅkarācārya
Jīva = Brahman. Pure non-dualism. Brahman is Nirguṇa (without attributes).
Viśiṣṭādvaita
Rāmānujācārya
Qualified non-dualism. Jīva is a part — Brahman is the whole. Saguṇa Brahman.
Dvaita
Madhvācārya
Jīva ≠ Brahman. Pure dualism. Viṣṇu is supreme — theistic devotion.
03
Linguistics — Pāṇini & Sanskrit
Aṣṭādhyāyī · Kāraka · Piṅgala binary · Articulation
📐Pāṇini's Aṣṭādhyāyī — at a Glance
8
Chapters (Adhyāyas)
32
Sections (8×4 pādas)
3,983
Sūtras
14
Māheśvara Sūtras
Sanskrit is derivational and rule-based — like a programming language. Words are generated step-by-step from roots + suffixes. This is why Pāṇini's grammar is studied today for natural language processing — it is generative and algorithmic.
👥Tri-Muni of Sanskrit Grammar
The three great grammarians. (Note: Piṅgala is not one of them — he did metrics.)
Pāṇini
Composed the Aṣṭādhyāyī — the foundational sūtra text
Kātyāyana
Wrote Vārttikas — annotations and refinements on Pāṇini's sūtras
Patañjali
Authored the Mahābhāṣya — the great commentary that ties it all together
🧩How Sanskrit Words Are Generated
Dhātupāṭha: the repository of ~2,200 verbal roots. 22 Upasargas (prefixes) can be added to verbs to strengthen, modify, or reverse their meaning. The process is recursive — vocabulary is generated, not fixed.
🎭The Kāraka System — Sentence Roles
1 · Kartā
The doer / agent
2 · Karma
The object / result
3 · Karaṇa
The instrument or tool
4 · Sampradāna
The receiver / destination
5 · Apādāna
The source / separation
6 · (Relation)
Not strictly a kāraka — possessive sense
7 · Adhikaraṇa
The locus / context
💻Piṅgala's Binary — 2000 Years Before de Bruijn
Laghu (short) = 1 · Guru (long) = 0. Three syllables → 2³ = 8 unique patterns called Ganas.
G
G
G
Ma
G
G
L
Ya
G
L
G
Ra
G
L
L
Bha
L
G
G
Ta
L
G
L
Ja
L
L
G
Sa
L
L
L
Na
Mnemonic: Yamātārājabhānasalagām — encodes the cycle of all 8 patterns. Anticipates the de Bruijn sequence by 2000+ years.
Uddiṣṭa: given binary → find row. Naṣṭa: given row → find binary.
👄Śikṣā — Places of Articulation
Kaṇṭha
Throat
ka · kha · ga
Tāla
Palate
cha · ja
Mūrdha
Upper palate
ṭa · ṭha · ḍa
Dantaḥ
Teeth
ta · tha (dental)
Oṣṭhau
Lips
pa · pha · ba · ma
04
Wisdom, Logic & Knowledge Framework
Pramāṇas · Knowledge triangle · Inference · Siddhānta
🔺The Knowledge Triangle
Every act of knowing has three components — and produces Pramā (valid cognition).
🔭Four Pramāṇas — Means of Valid Knowledge
👁️
Pratyakṣa
Direct perception
"I see fire."
💨
Anumāna
Inference
"I see smoke → there is fire."
🔁
Upamāna
Comparison
"A nilgai is like a cow."
📚
Śabda
Testimony
Reliable source / scripture / expert
🧠Five-Step Logic (Anumāna · Nyāya)
The classical inference structure — example: proving sound is non-eternal.
1
Pratijñā
Hypothesis
"Sound is non-eternal"
→
2
Hetu
Reason
"because it is produced"
→
3
Udāharaṇa
General rule + example
"whatever is produced is non-eternal, like a pot"
→
4
Upanaya
Application
"sound is produced like a pot"
→
5
Nigamana
Conclusion
"therefore, sound is non-eternal"
🌀The Knowledge Creation Process
Prayojana — the purpose that drives inquiry
↓
Saṃśaya — doubt arises (the start of investigation)
↓
Vāda — debate (goal: establish truth, not defeat opponent)
↓
Siddhānta — established tenet emerges
↓
Nirṇaya — final confirmed conclusion
Vitaṇḍā = refuting without making your own claim. Hetvābhāsa = five logical fallacies (erratic, opposite, circular, unproved, mistimed).
📐Four Types of Siddhānta
Sarva-tantra
Accepted by all schools (e.g. five senses, causality)
Prati-tantra
Unique to one school or domain (e.g. F=ma in physics, Tridoṣa in Āyurveda)
Adhikaraṇa
If proven, implies others (e.g. water exists → living beings exist)
Abhyupagama
Hypothetical assumption for investigation (e.g. Euclid's postulates)
📖Sources of Ancestral Wisdom
Purāṇas
Encyclopaedic — stories + philosophy + history. 18 Mahā-purāṇas + 18 Upa-purāṇas. Five characteristics (Pañca-lakṣaṇa): Sarga · Pratisarga · Vaṃśa · Manvantara · Vaṃśānucarita
Itihāsas
"Thus it happened." Rāmāyaṇa (Sūrya-vaṃśa, normative) and Mahābhārata (Chandra-vaṃśa, descriptive). Focus on the four Puruṣārthas.
Nīti-śāstra
"That which leads/guides." Pañcatantra (Viṣṇu Śarmā) · Vidura Nīti.
Subhāṣita
Beautifully rendered verses carrying wisdom.
⚔️Rāmāyaṇa vs Mahābhārata
Rāmāyaṇa
  • Sūrya-vaṃśa (solar dynasty)
  • Normative — ideal behaviour
  • Vālmīki narrates from within the story
Mahābhārata
  • Chandra-vaṃśa (lunar dynasty)
  • Descriptive — moral grey zones
  • Vyāsa narrates from within the story
  • Home of complex ethical dilemmas
05
Number Systems, Math & Astronomy
Bhūta-saṃkhyā · Kaṭapayādi · Pañcāṅga · Yantras
🔢Two Encoding Systems — Two Reading Rules
Bhūta-saṃkhyā

Uses objects to represent numbers.

Rule: extract numbers → read right to left

Kaṭapayādi

Associates Sanskrit consonants with digits 0–9 (ka, ṭa, pa, ya groups). Vowels alone = 0.

Rule: decipher → then reverse the sequence

🗝️Bhūta-saṃkhyā — Common Mappings
0
Śūnya
(void)
1
Candra
(moon)
2
Netra
(eyes)
3
Rāma
Agni · Guṇa
4
Veda
Yuga · Varṇa
5
Pāṇḍava
Bhūta
7
Svara
Dhātu
8
Gaja
Ahi
9
Nanda
12
Rāśi
Āditya
Subtractive principle (Ekona = "one less than"): Ekonatriṃśat = 30 − 1 = 29 · Ekona-catvāriṃśat = 40 − 1 = 39
Parārdha = 10¹⁷ · Zero formalised by Brahmagupta (628 CE)
🕰️Indian Mathematicians — Timeline
476 CE
Āryabhaṭa
Sine tables, place value, π ≈ 3.1416, earth rotates on axis
628 CE
Brahmagupta
Formalised zero. Rules for negative numbers.
1114 CE
Bhāskara II
Līlāvatī (arithmetic) · Bījaganita (algebra)
1340 CE
Mādhava
Infinite series for π, sin, cos (Kerala school)
Modern
Ramanujan
π to 17 million places, magic squares, number theory
📏Geometry — The Pythagorean Theorem in India
Bhuja-koṭi-karṇa-nyāya
First documented in the Baudhāyana Śulba Sūtra. Used to construct sacred altars. Śulba Sūtras were composed by multiple authors — Baudhāyana, Āpastamba, Mānava — for rope geometry.
Constructing a circular altar with the same area as a square altar required early understanding of π and geometric transformations.
📅Pañcāṅga — The Five-Limb Calendar
Tithi
12° angular separation between Sun and Moon
Karaṇa
Half-tithi — 6° separation
Nakṣatra
Moon's position across 27 divisions
Yoga
Sum of Sun + Moon longitudes (multiples of 13°20')
Vāra
Day of the week
Adhika Māsa: extra lunar month inserted every ~2.5 years to bridge the 11-day gap between solar and lunar years.
🌞Celestial Concepts
Uttarāyaṇa
Sun moves north → longer days in the Northern Hemisphere
Dakṣiṇāyana
Sun moves south → shorter days in the Northern Hemisphere
Viṣuvat
Equinoxes — equal day and night
Zenith
The point directly above the observer
Kṛṣṇa Pakṣa
Waning fortnight — Pūrṇimā → Amāvasyā
Ecliptic tilt
23.5° — the axial tilt of the Earth
Lunar month
Moon returns to the same position relative to Sun (synodic)
Solar month
Sun moves from one Rāśi to the next
🔭Astronomical Instruments (Yantras)
Cakra-yantra
Determines longitude and latitude of planets
Turīya-yantra
Determines zenith distance and latitude
Nāḍīvalaya
Timings of rising and setting (Lagna)
Śaṅku (Gnomon)
Cardinal directions and time of day
Ghaṭī-yantra
Water clock
06
Engineering & Technology
Iron · Zinc · Wootz · Lost wax · Water
🏛️Three Pillars of Evidence
How we know what ancient Indian technology was capable of.
⛏️
Archaeological
Excavated sites — Kalibangan, Lothal, Bhimbetka
🏛️
Living Examples
Standing structures — Delhi Iron Pillar, Thanjavur Temple
📜
Literary Sources
Texts describing techniques — Rasa-Ratna-Samuccaya, Yukti-kalpataru
⚒️Metallurgical Achievements
🗿
Delhi Iron Pillar
6,000 kg · ~2,000 years old · still corrosion-resistant (high-phosphorus iron)
⚔️
Wootz Steel
From ~700 BCE · used for Damascus blades · 4–6 hour process
⚗️
Zinc Extraction
India was the global pioneer · 600–200 BCE · Zawar, Rajasthan · downward-drift distillation (Rasa-Ratna-Samuccaya)
🕯️
Lost-Wax Casting
Madhūcchiṣṭa Vidhānam — "residue of beehives" · beeswax pattern
🪙
Pañcaloha
A 5-metal alloy: Gold · Silver · Lead · Copper · Zinc
🏯
Bronze Icons
Chola-period idols — high point of Indian alloying technology
💧Water Management
Dholāvīrā
Indus Valley · 16 reservoirs · systematic urban planning
Kallanai (Grand Anicut)
Built by Karikalan Chola (1st C CE) — oldest dam still in use
Step-wells (Vāvs)
Gujarat & Rajasthan — seasonal water storage
Ahar-Pyne
South Bihar (Mauryan period) — rainwater harvesting
🏥Suśruta & Salya-tantra
Śalya-tantra = surgical science. Suśruta described 101 blunt + 20 sharp surgical instruments. Procedures included rhinoplasty, cataract removal, and treatment of urinary calculi. Operating theatres were fumigated with guggulu for sterilisation.
⛵Shipbuilding & Jāti
Three water routes
Arthaśāstra classifies them as Kulya, Kulapatha, Samyānapatha.
Ship design
Vessels up to 50.27 m. Iron fastenings avoided due to feared magnetic effects.
Jāti — original sense
Skilled knowledge passed within communities — guild-like, not rigid hierarchy.
07
Town Planning & Governance
Vāstu · Saptāṅga · Town designs · Rāja-dharma
🛕Aṣṭāṅga of Vāstu — The Eight Limbs
The eight components that together make up Vāstu-vidyā.
Important: Jyotiṣa is not one of the 8 limbs — it is a Vedāṅga.
👷The Architectural Team
Sthapati
Master architect — the leader
Sūtragrāhin
Draftsman / measurer
Takṣaka
Stone-cutter and carver
Vardhakin
Joins parts and refines/polishes surfaces
🟦Vāstu Puruṣa Maṇḍala
The site plan is viewed as a living entity. The center — Brahmasthala — aligns with cosmic energy and must remain unobstructed.
🏯Temple Architecture
Adhiṣṭhāna
Base platform
Garbhagṛha
Sanctum sanctorum
Maṇḍapa
Pillared halls
Prākāra
Circumambulation space
Vimāna / Śikhara
Tower / dome
Nāgara (North)

Curving beehive-shaped Śikhara.

Drāviḍa (South)

Stepped pyramid-shaped Vimāna.

🗺️Town Design Patterns
Daṇḍaka
Parallel streets · for retirees / priests
Sarvatobhadra
Safe from all sides (e.g. Chandigarh)
Kārmukha
Bow-shaped · coastal settlements
Prastāra
Couch-shaped (e.g. Jaipur)
Maṭha / Vihāra
University town
👑Saptāṅga — Seven Elements of State (Arthaśāstra)
Kauṭilya's framework, ~300 BCE. Strikingly modern parallels.
1 · Svāmī
King / leader
≈ CEO
2 · Amātya
Minister (executive)
≈ CXOs
3 · Janapada
Territory / people
≈ Markets / Stakeholders
4 · Durga
Fort / capital
≈ IP / Patents
5 · Kośa
Treasury
≈ Finance
6 · Daṇḍa
Army / justice
≈ Compliance / Audit
7 · Mitra
Ally · ≈ JV Partners
Kośa > Daṇḍa — treasury is more important than army.
Amātya vs Mantri: Amātyas execute; Mantris are a small group of secret advisors.
🤝Ṣāḍguṇya — Six Methods of Foreign Policy
Sandhi
Peace / treaty
Vigraha
Hostility
Āsana
Remaining quiet
Yāna
Military expedition
Saṃśraya
Seeking shelter
Dvaidhībhāva
Double policy
Overcoming opposition (in order): Sāma (conciliation) → Dāna (gifts) → Bheda (dissension) → Daṇḍa (force)
⚖️Rāja-dharma — Duties of the Ruler
The king is regarded as a representative of God, responsible for upholding Dharma. He must prevent Matsya-nyāya ("the law of the fish" — anarchy where the big eat the small). Taxation should be like milking a cow — sustained, never harming the source.

Governance discussed in: Śānti Parva (Mahābhārata) and Kaschitta Sarga (Rāmāyaṇa).
08
Health, Wellness & Psychology
Tri-doṣa · Sapta-dhātu · Pañca-koṣa · Four states
🌱Definition of Health (Svastha)
Physical Balance
  • Balanced Doṣas
  • Balanced Agni (digestive fire)
  • Balanced Dhātus (tissues)
  • Proper Mala (waste elimination)
Psychological Balance
  • Pleasant Ātma (soul)
  • Pleasant Indriya (senses)
  • Pleasant Manas (mind)
"Sama" means balanced according to the individual's constitution — not equal proportions for everyone. Each person's Prakṛti is unique.
🧍Tri-doṣa — Located in the Body
Each doṣa governs a specific region and combines two of the five elements.
HEAD ↔ CHEST
Kapha
Water + Earth · stability, structure, moisture
CHEST ↔ NAVEL
Pitta
Fire + Water · digestion, warmth, transformation
BELOW NAVEL
Vāta
Air + Space · motion, energy, breath
Dominance shifts by time and age: Morning / childhood = Kapha · Noon / youth = Pitta · Evening / old age = Vāta
🧬Sapta-dhātu — Seven Tissues (Transformation Chain)
Rasa
Plasma
→
Rakta
Blood
→
Māṃsa
Flesh
→
Medas
Fat
→
Asthi
Bone
→
Majjā
Marrow
→
Śukra
Reproductive
→
Ojas
Vital essence
Agni (digestive fire) manifests through Pitta and converts food into energy and tissues. Low Agni → constipation; high Agni → cooks tissues and doṣas.
🌅Dinacaryā — Daily Regimen
Brāhma-muhūrta
Wake 2 muhūrtas (~96 mins) before sunrise. Vāta-dominant — best for mental clarity.
Uṣā-pāna
Drink ~1 litre of warm water while sitting.
Abhyaṅga
Daily oil massage — head, ears, legs.
Exercise + Bath
Both are daily essentials.
Food rules: Guru (heavy) foods — eat only to half of hunger. Laghu (light) foods — until full. 3-hour (1 Yāma) rule: food eaten beyond 3 hours of cooking becomes Tāmasa.
📚The Two Tripods of Āyurveda
Bṛhat Trāyi — The Major Three
  • Caraka Saṃhitā
  • Suśruta Saṃhitā
  • Aṣṭāṅga Hṛdaya
Laghu Trāyi — The Minor Three
  • Mādhava Nidhāna
  • Śāraṅgadhara Saṃhitā
  • Bhāvaprakāśa
🌟The Core of Indian Psychology
Sat · Chit · Ānanda
Existence · Consciousness · Bliss
Every human being is, in essence, Sat-Chit-Ānanda. Suffering arises from ignorance of one's true nature; liberation = realising it.
🪆Pañca-koṣa — The Five Sheaths
The self is wrapped in five layers — and is distinct from all of them, like a person from their clothes.
1 · Annamaya
Physical / food body — outermost
2 · Prāṇamaya
Vital energy / breath
3 · Manomaya
Mental — thoughts and feelings
4 · Vijñānamaya
Intellectual — knowledge / discernment
5 · Ānandamaya
Bliss — closest to true self
⊙ Ātman
The unchanging witness — distinct from all coverings
🧠Antaḥkaraṇa — The Internal Instrument
The cognitive flow: how a stimulus becomes a decision.
Sense Organ
receives stimulus
→
Manas
processes sensory input
→
Citta
repository of past experiences
→
Ahaṃkāra
agency / "I" sense
→
Buddhi
decision / evaluation
🌑Four States of Consciousness (Avasthās)
Jāgrat
Waking
External engagement · gross + subtle body active
Svapna
Dream
Internal world creation · subtle body only
Suṣupti
Deep Sleep
Peace without awareness · neither body active
Turīya
The Fourth
Pure witness consciousness — transcends all three
🧘Practices for Inner Focus
Vairāgya
Dispassion / detachment — reduces distractions from the external world.
Abhyāsa
Constant practice — reinforces inner focus over time.
Three-stage path of realisation: Śravaṇa (hearing) → Manana (reflection) → Nididhyāsana (direct experience)
🎨Tri-guṇa in Psychology
Just as R, G, B combine to produce all colours, Sattva-Rajas-Tamas combine in different proportions to explain the diversity of human behaviour despite a shared metaphysical essence.

That's the whole syllabus — visualised. 🪷

Use this view for last-mile revision. For full lessons and MCQs, head back home.

Crafted by Harsha Vardhan.

`; printableCache.set(cacheKey, html); return html; } function openPrintWindow(html) { const existingFrame = document.getElementById('printFrame'); if (existingFrame) existingFrame.remove(); const frame = document.createElement('iframe'); frame.id = 'printFrame'; frame.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); frame.style.position = 'fixed'; frame.style.right = '0'; frame.style.bottom = '0'; frame.style.width = '0'; frame.style.height = '0'; frame.style.border = '0'; frame.style.opacity = '0'; document.body.appendChild(frame); let cleanupScheduled = false; const cleanup = (delay = 0) => { if (cleanupScheduled) return; cleanupScheduled = true; setTimeout(() => { const currentFrame = document.getElementById('printFrame'); if (currentFrame) currentFrame.remove(); }, delay); }; frame.onload = () => { const targetWindow = frame.contentWindow; if (!targetWindow) { cleanup(); toast('Print preview could not open. Please try again.'); return; } let printed = false; const triggerPrint = () => { if (printed) return; printed = true; try { targetWindow.focus(); targetWindow.print(); toast('Opening print dialog...'); } catch (err) { toast('Print dialog was blocked. Try Cmd+P after the print sheet opens.'); cleanup(1000); } }; targetWindow.onafterprint = () => cleanup(1500); // Keep the print frame alive long enough for long macOS previews to fully paginate. setTimeout(() => cleanup(0), 120000); setTimeout(triggerPrint, 500); }; try { const doc = frame.contentDocument || frame.contentWindow.document; doc.open(); doc.write(html); doc.close(); } catch (err) { cleanup(); const printWindow = window.open('', '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer'); if (!printWindow) { toast('Print view could not open automatically. Please allow pop-ups or try again.'); return; } printWindow.document.open(); printWindow.document.write(html); printWindow.document.close(); setTimeout(() => { try { printWindow.focus(); printWindow.print(); } catch (popupErr) { toast('Print view opened. If needed, press Cmd+P.'); } }, 700); } } function printModulePack(moduleId) { toast('Preparing print view...'); setTimeout(() => openPrintWindow(buildPrintableHTML([moduleId])), 30); } function printAllModulesPack() { toast('Preparing print pack for all modules...'); setTimeout(() => openPrintWindow(buildPrintableHTML(getUnlockedModuleIds())), 30); } function renderHome() { state.currentModule = null; $('backBtn').classList.remove('show'); $('brandText').textContent = 'IKS · 8 Modules'; $('scorePill').classList.remove('show'); $('streakPill').classList.remove('show'); $('progressTrack').classList.remove('show'); $('moduleTracker').classList.remove('show'); let cardsHTML = ''; for (let i = 1; i <= 8; i++) { const m = moduleMeta[i]; const progress = state.moduleProgress[i]; const locked = !m.unlocked; const completed = progress && progress.grade; const tierClass = locked ? '' : (i <= 4 ? 'tier-color-1' : 'tier-color-2'); const classes = ['module-card', tierClass, locked ? 'locked' : '', completed ? 'completed' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' '); const statusBadge = locked ? '🔒 Coming soon' : (completed ? '✓ Completed' : '● Ready'); const footRight = locked ? 'Locked' : `
`; let bestScoreBadge = ''; if (completed) { bestScoreBadge = 'Best: ' + progress.grade + ' · ' + progress.accuracy + '%'; if (progress.lastGrade && progress.attempts > 1) { bestScoreBadge += '
Last: ' + progress.lastGrade + ' · ' + progress.lastAccuracy + '% · ' + progress.attempts + ' attempts
'; } } else { bestScoreBadge = m.lessonCount ? '' + m.lessonCount + ' lessons · ' + m.mcqCount + ' MCQs' : ''; } const clickHandler = locked ? '' : (completed ? '' : `onclick="enterModule(${i})"`); if (completed) { // ===== ACHIEVEMENT SCORECARD ===== const p = progress; const pct = p.bestAccuracy || p.accuracy || 0; let achBadge = '💪', achText = 'LEARNER', achPill = 'bp-default', achBg = 'achievement-bg-default', gradeClass = 'ach-grade-default'; if (pct >= 90) { achBadge = '🏆'; achText = 'MASTER'; achPill = 'bp-master'; achBg = 'achievement-bg-master'; gradeClass = 'ach-grade-master'; } else if (pct >= 75) { achBadge = '🥇'; achText = 'EXPERT'; achPill = 'bp-expert'; achBg = 'achievement-bg-expert'; } else if (pct >= 60) { achBadge = '🥈'; achText = 'SCHOLAR'; achPill = 'bp-scholar'; achBg = 'achievement-bg-scholar'; } else if (pct >= 40) { achBadge = '🥉'; achText = 'APPRENTICE'; achPill = 'bp-default'; } cardsHTML += `
${achBadge}
${p.bestGrade || p.grade}
${achText}
${m.emoji}
Module ${i} · ${escapeHtml(m.title)}
${escapeHtml(m.subtitle)}
${p.bestScore || p.lastScore || 0}Points
${pct}%Accuracy
${p.bestStreak || p.lastStreak || 0}Streak
${p.attempts || 1}${(p.attempts||1)===1?'Attempt':'Attempts'}
`; } else { // ===== NORMAL CARD (not completed) ===== cardsHTML += `
${i} ${m.emoji}
${statusBadge}

${escapeHtml(m.title)}

${escapeHtml(m.subtitle)}

${bestScoreBadge} ${footRight}
`; } } const totalReadyMCQs = Object.values(moduleMeta).filter(m => m.unlocked).reduce((s, m) => s + (m.mcqCount || 0), 0); const totalReadyLessons = Object.values(moduleMeta).filter(m => m.unlocked).reduce((s, m) => s + (m.lessonCount || 0), 0); $('screen').innerHTML = `
IIM Bangalore · BBA DBE · Indian Knowledge Systems

Indian Knowledge Systems
your one-stop study material.

Built for the night before the exam, the week before, or whenever you actually decide to start. Every module is tier-ranked (must-know · very likely · application), every MCQ mirrors a real PYQ pattern, and every correct answer earns you real crowd applause and confetti. Pick a module to begin — your best score saves automatically.

${totalReadyLessons} lessons · ${totalReadyMCQs} MCQs · all 8 modules live ✨

Printable revision pack: concepts first, all MCQs next, and the full answer key at the end in reverse order.

⭐ EXAM-DAY HACK · ONE-SHOT REVISION
Every must-know exam concept across all modules — distilled into diagrams, flow-charts, color-coded panels and mnemonics. The perfect last-look before you walk into the exam hall. Tap to open.
Choose your module
${cardsHTML}
`; window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }); } function enterModule(moduleId) { if (!moduleMeta[moduleId] || !moduleMeta[moduleId].unlocked) return; // Reset per-module state state.currentModule = moduleId; state.score = 0; state.streak = 0; state.bestStreak = 0; state.correctCount = 0; state.totalAnswered = 0; state.completedLessons = new Set(); state.answeredQuestions = new Set(); state.wrongTopics = new Map(); // Update topbar $('backBtn').classList.add('show'); $('brandText').textContent = `IKS · Module ${moduleId}`; $('scorePill').classList.add('show'); $('streakPill').classList.add('show'); $('progressTrack').classList.add('show'); renderModule(moduleId); updateModuleTracker(); window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }); } function goHome() { $('moduleTracker').classList.remove('show'); hideCheatsheet(); renderHome(); } function renderModule(moduleId) { const meta = moduleMeta[moduleId]; const lessons = lessonsByModule[moduleId]; if (!lessons) return; let total = 0, lessonsHTML = ''; const tierMeta = { 1: { emoji: '🔥', label: 'TIER 1 · MUST KNOW', sub: "Core content. Master this and you're safe.", tag: 'tag-mandatory', tagText: 'Mandatory' }, 2: { emoji: '⚡', label: 'TIER 2 · VERY LIKELY', sub: 'Almost certain to appear.', tag: 'tag-likely', tagText: 'Very likely' }, 3: { emoji: '🎯', label: 'TIER 3 · APPLICATION', sub: "Scenario-based. The kind they love to test.", tag: 'tag-bonus', tagText: 'Application' } }; const tierMotivation = { 1: 'Start strong. Every lesson here matters.', 2: "You're building momentum. Each lesson stacks on the last.", 3: 'Final stretch. Apply what you\'ve learned to scenarios.' }; let lastTier = 0; lessons.forEach((lesson, idx) => { if (lesson.tier !== lastTier) { const tm = tierMeta[lesson.tier]; lessonsHTML += `
${tm.emoji}

${tm.label}

${tm.sub}

`; if (lesson.tier > 1) lessonsHTML += `
${tierMotivation[lesson.tier]}
`; lastTier = lesson.tier; } let questionsHTML = ''; lesson.questions.forEach((q, qIdx) => { total++; const qId = `q-${lesson.id}-${qIdx}`; questionsHTML += `
Practice ${qIdx + 1} of ${lesson.questions.length}
${q.q}
`; const letters = ['A','B','C','D','E']; questionsHTML += `
`; q.options.forEach((opt, oIdx) => { questionsHTML += ``; }); questionsHTML += `
`; questionsHTML += `
`; }); const tm = tierMeta[lesson.tier]; lessonsHTML += `
${lesson.id}

${lesson.title}

○ 0/${lesson.questions.length} ${tm.tagText}
${lesson.body}${questionsHTML}
`; if ((idx + 1) % 3 === 0 && idx + 1 < lessons.length) { const nudgeMsgs = ['Three lessons down. Stretch, breathe, keep going.', "You're building real understanding. Don't rush.", 'Small wins every few minutes add up.', 'Hydrate. Then back to it.']; lessonsHTML += `
${pick(nudgeMsgs)}
`; } }); state.totalQuestions = total; $('screen').innerHTML = `
Module ${moduleId} · ${escapeHtml(meta.title)}

${escapeHtml(meta.title)}
made unmissable.

${escapeHtml(meta.heroSub)}

"${escapeHtml(meta.motivation)}"
${lessonsHTML}
Module ${moduleId} · Final grade
🏆
A
A
MASTER

Outstanding!

You're ready.

0
Total Points
0
Correct
0%
Accuracy
0
Best Streak

`; updateUI(); } // ===== MCQ HANDLER ===== function handleMCQ(btn) { const lessonId = parseInt(btn.dataset.lesson); const qIdx = parseInt(btn.dataset.q); const choice = parseInt(btn.dataset.choice); const answer = parseInt(btn.dataset.answer); const qId = `q-${lessonId}-${qIdx}`; if (state.answeredQuestions.has(qId)) return; state.answeredQuestions.add(qId); const allButtons = btn.parentElement.querySelectorAll('.opt'); allButtons.forEach((b, i) => { b.disabled = true; if (i === answer) b.classList.add(i === choice ? 'correct' : 'disabled-correct'); if (i === choice && i !== answer) b.classList.add('wrong'); }); const correct = choice === answer; const lessons = lessonsByModule[state.currentModule]; const lesson = lessons.find(l => l.id === lessonId); showFeedback(qId, correct, lesson.questions[qIdx].explain, lessonId); awardPoints(correct, btn); checkLessonDone(lessonId); } function showFeedback(qId, correct, explanation, lessonId) { const fb = $(qId + '-feedback'); fb.classList.add('show', correct ? 'good' : 'bad'); const head = correct ? pick(nudges.correct) : pick(nudges.wrong); fb.innerHTML = `
${correct ? '✓' : '✗'} ${head}
${explanation}
`; if (!correct) state.wrongTopics.set(lessonId, (state.wrongTopics.get(lessonId) || 0) + 1); } function awardPoints(correct, btn) { state.totalAnswered++; const rect = btn.getBoundingClientRect(); const cx = rect.left + rect.width / 2, cy = rect.top; if (correct) { state.streak++; state.bestStreak = Math.max(state.bestStreak, state.streak); state.correctCount++; let pts = 10; if (state.streak >= 10) pts += 20; else if (state.streak >= 5) pts += 10; else if (state.streak >= 3) pts += 5; state.score += pts; floatPoint(cx, cy, `+${pts}`); dualConfetti(cx, cy); sndDragon(); pulse('scorePill'); if (state.streak === 3) { toast(pick(nudges.streak3)); sndDragon(0.9); } else if (state.streak === 5) { toast(pick(nudges.streak5)); sndDragon(); bigConfetti(); } else if (state.streak === 10) { toast(pick(nudges.streak10)); sndDragon(); bigConfetti(); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 600); } } else { state.streak = 0; state.score = Math.max(0, state.score - 2); floatPoint(cx, cy, '-2', true); sndWrong(); } updateUI(); if (state.totalAnswered === Math.floor(state.totalQuestions / 2)) setTimeout(() => toast(pick(nudges.midway)), 600); } function checkLessonDone(lessonId) { const lessons = lessonsByModule[state.currentModule]; const lesson = lessons.find(l => l.id === lessonId); const allQids = lesson.questions.map((_, i) => `q-${lessonId}-${i}`); const done = allQids.every(qid => state.answeredQuestions.has(qid)); if (done && !state.completedLessons.has(lessonId)) { state.completedLessons.add(lessonId); const lessonEl = $(`lesson-${lessonId}`); if (lessonEl) { lessonEl.classList.add('done'); const numText = lessonEl.querySelector('.num-text'); if (numText) numText.textContent = '✓'; } setTimeout(() => toast(pick(nudges.lessonDone)), 1100); setTimeout(() => sndApplauseShort(1.8, 0.75), 1250); // crowd applause short burst — lesson complete const tier = lesson.tier; const allTierLessons = lessons.filter(l => l.tier === tier); const tierDone = allTierLessons.every(l => state.completedLessons.has(l.id)); if (tierDone) setTimeout(() => { toast(pick(nudges.tierDone)); bigConfetti(); sndDragon(); }, 2000); } if (state.completedLessons.size === lessons.length) setTimeout(showFinal, 2500); } function showFinal() { const pct = Math.round(state.totalAnswered ? (state.correctCount / state.totalAnswered) * 100 : 0); let grade = 'D', msg = "Keep working — review topics you missed.", sub = "Focus on weak areas."; if (pct >= 90) { grade = 'A+'; msg = "Outstanding! You're absolutely ready."; sub = "Walk in confident."; } else if (pct >= 80) { grade = 'A'; msg = "Excellent. You've got this nailed."; sub = "One more pass on tricky bits."; } else if (pct >= 70) { grade = 'B+'; msg = "Strong work. Solid foundation."; sub = "Skim weak topics one more time."; } else if (pct >= 60) { grade = 'B'; msg = "Decent. You'll do well."; sub = "Spend a bit more time on the lessons below."; } else if (pct >= 50) { grade = 'C'; msg = "On the edge — push through weak topics."; sub = "Re-do where you got stuck."; } else { grade = 'D'; msg = "Don't panic. There's still time."; sub = "Pick the top 5 weak topics below and re-read."; } $('gradeLetter').textContent = grade; $('gradeMsg').textContent = msg; $('gradeSub').textContent = sub; $('finalScore').textContent = state.score; $('finalCorrect').textContent = `${state.correctCount}/${state.totalAnswered}`; $('finalAcc').textContent = pct + '%'; $('finalStreak').textContent = state.bestStreak; // Persist per-module scorecard (this attempt + all-time best are tracked separately) const moduleId = state.currentModule; const prev = state.moduleProgress[moduleId] || {}; const newScore = state.score; const isNewBest = !prev.bestScore || newScore > prev.bestScore; const attempts = (prev.attempts || 0) + 1; // Always store this attempt's result so the scorecard reflects the latest run state.moduleProgress[moduleId] = { // All-time best (only updates when score improves) bestScore: isNewBest ? newScore : prev.bestScore, bestGrade: isNewBest ? grade : prev.bestGrade, bestAccuracy: isNewBest ? pct : prev.bestAccuracy, bestStreak: Math.max(prev.bestStreak || 0, state.bestStreak), // This attempt lastScore: newScore, lastGrade: grade, lastAccuracy: pct, lastStreak: state.bestStreak, // Meta attempts: attempts, completedAt: new Date().toISOString(), // For home page badge — keep the older "grade" + "accuracy" keys pointing to best grade: isNewBest ? grade : prev.bestGrade, accuracy: isNewBest ? pct : prev.bestAccuracy }; saveProgress(); const lessons = lessonsByModule[moduleId]; if (state.wrongTopics.size > 0) { const sorted = [...state.wrongTopics.entries()].sort((a,b) => b[1] - a[1]).slice(0, 5); const topicNames = sorted.map(([id]) => { const l = lessons.find(x => x.id === id); return l ? l.title : ''; }).filter(Boolean); if (topicNames.length) $('weakTopics').innerHTML = 'Revisit:
' + topicNames.join(' · '); } // ===== Show "personal best" comparison if this isn't their first attempt ===== if (attempts > 1 && !isNewBest) { const bestEl = document.createElement('div'); bestEl.style.cssText = 'background:rgba(255,255,255,0.6);padding:0.9rem 1.2rem;border-radius:14px;margin:1rem auto 0;max-width:380px;font-size:0.92rem;font-style:italic;font-family:Fraunces,serif;color:var(--ink-soft);'; bestEl.innerHTML = `Your personal best for this module: ${prev.bestGrade} · ${prev.bestScore} pts · ${prev.bestAccuracy}% (attempt ${attempts})`; $('weakTopics').parentNode.insertBefore(bestEl, $('weakTopics')); } else if (isNewBest && attempts > 1) { const newBestEl = document.createElement('div'); newBestEl.style.cssText = 'background:linear-gradient(135deg,#FFF0A8,#FFD4B8);padding:0.9rem 1.2rem;border-radius:14px;margin:1rem auto 0;max-width:380px;font-size:0.95rem;font-weight:700;font-family:Fraunces,serif;color:var(--ink);'; newBestEl.innerHTML = `🏆 New personal best! (attempt ${attempts})`; $('weakTopics').parentNode.insertBefore(newBestEl, $('weakTopics')); } // Build final actions: rich end-of-module navigation const nextId = moduleId + 1; const nextMeta = moduleMeta[nextId]; let actionsHTML = ''; // Primary: Next module (if unlocked) OR coming-soon note if (nextMeta && nextMeta.unlocked) { actionsHTML += ``; } else if (nextMeta && !nextMeta.unlocked) { actionsHTML += `
${nextMeta.emoji} Coming soon
Module ${nextId} · ${escapeHtml(nextMeta.title)} 🔒
`; } // Secondary actions: Retry + Back to home actionsHTML += `
`; $('finalActions').innerHTML = actionsHTML; // ===== BADGE SYSTEM ===== let badgeEmoji = '💪', badgeText = 'LEARNER', badgeClass = 'badge-learner'; if (pct >= 90) { badgeEmoji = '🏆'; badgeText = 'MASTER'; badgeClass = 'badge-master'; } else if (pct >= 75) { badgeEmoji = '🥇'; badgeText = 'EXPERT'; badgeClass = 'badge-expert'; } else if (pct >= 60) { badgeEmoji = '🥈'; badgeText = 'SCHOLAR'; badgeClass = 'badge-scholar'; } else if (pct >= 40) { badgeEmoji = '🥉'; badgeText = 'APPRENTICE'; badgeClass = 'badge-learner'; } $('gradeBadge').textContent = badgeEmoji; $('gradeGlow').textContent = grade; const bl = $('badgeLabel'); bl.textContent = badgeText; bl.className = 'badge-label ' + badgeClass; // ===== STAR PARTICLES in the card ===== const starsEl = $('starsRain'); starsEl.innerHTML = ''; const starEmojis = ['✨','⭐','🌟','💫','✦','★']; for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) { const s = document.createElement('span'); s.className = 'star-particle'; s.textContent = starEmojis[Math.floor(Math.random()*starEmojis.length)]; s.style.left = Math.random()*100 + '%'; s.style.top = Math.random()*40 + '%'; s.style.animationDelay = (Math.random()*2) + 's'; s.style.fontSize = (0.8 + Math.random()*1.2) + 'rem'; starsEl.appendChild(s); } $('finalCard').classList.add('show'); $('finalCard').scrollIntoView({behavior: 'smooth'}); // ===== SCORECARD CELEBRATION — trombone × 2 + confetti ===== sndTrombone(); bigConfetti(); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 500); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 1000); setTimeout(() => sndTrombone(0.85), 1800); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 1800); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 2500); // Extra confetti for high grades (no extra sound) if (pct >= 80) { setTimeout(bigConfetti, 3200); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 3900); } if (pct >= 90) { setTimeout(bigConfetti, 4600); setTimeout(bigConfetti, 5300); } } // ===== SHARE MODULE — generates a beautiful card image ===== // roundRect polyfill for older browsers if (!CanvasRenderingContext2D.prototype.roundRect) { CanvasRenderingContext2D.prototype.roundRect = function(x, y, w, h, r) { if (typeof r === 'number') r = [r,r,r,r]; this.moveTo(x+r[0], y); this.lineTo(x+w-r[1], y); this.quadraticCurveTo(x+w, y, x+w, y+r[1]); this.lineTo(x+w, y+h-r[2]); this.quadraticCurveTo(x+w, y+h, x+w-r[2], y+h); this.lineTo(x+r[3], y+h); this.quadraticCurveTo(x, y+h, x, y+h-r[3]); this.lineTo(x, y+r[0]); this.quadraticCurveTo(x, y, x+r[0], y); this.closePath(); }; } async function shareModule(moduleId) { const m = moduleMeta[moduleId]; const p = state.moduleProgress[moduleId]; if (!p) return; const pct = p.bestAccuracy || p.accuracy || 0; const grade = p.bestGrade || p.grade || '?'; const score = p.bestScore || p.lastScore || 0; const streak = p.bestStreak || p.lastStreak || 0; let badge = '💪', badgeText = 'LEARNER'; if (pct >= 90) { badge = '🏆'; badgeText = 'MASTER'; } else if (pct >= 75) { badge = '🥇'; badgeText = 'EXPERT'; } else if (pct >= 60) { badge = '🥈'; badgeText = 'SCHOLAR'; } else if (pct >= 40) { badge = '🥉'; badgeText = 'APPRENTICE'; } // Create canvas const W = 1080, H = 1080; const c = document.createElement('canvas'); c.width = W; c.height = H; const ctx = c.getContext('2d'); // Background gradient const grad = ctx.createLinearGradient(0, 0, W, H); if (pct >= 90) { grad.addColorStop(0, '#FFF8E1'); grad.addColorStop(0.5, '#FFE0B2'); grad.addColorStop(1, '#FFF0A8'); } else if (pct >= 75) { grad.addColorStop(0, '#F5F5F5'); grad.addColorStop(0.5, '#E8E8E8'); grad.addColorStop(1, '#F0F0F0'); } else if (pct >= 60) { grad.addColorStop(0, '#FFF3E0'); grad.addColorStop(0.5, '#FFE0C0'); grad.addColorStop(1, '#FFF0D6'); } else { grad.addColorStop(0, '#E3F2FD'); grad.addColorStop(0.5, '#C2DFFF'); grad.addColorStop(1, '#F0F7FF'); } ctx.fillStyle = grad; ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H); // Subtle pattern dots ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.03)'; for (let x = 0; x < W; x += 30) for (let y = 0; y < H; y += 30) { ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(x, y, 2, 0, Math.PI*2); ctx.fill(); } // Top label ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.45)'; ctx.font = '700 22px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; ctx.fillText('IIM BANGALORE · BBA DBE · IKS', W/2, 80); // Badge emoji (drawn as text) ctx.font = '160px serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; ctx.fillText(badge, W/2, 280); // Grade letter ctx.fillStyle = pct >= 90 ? '#FF7A72' : '#2D2A26'; ctx.font = '900 140px "Fraunces", Georgia, serif'; ctx.fillText(grade, W/2, 440); // Badge text pill const pillW = ctx.measureText(badgeText).width; ctx.font = '800 26px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; const tw = ctx.measureText(badgeText).width; ctx.fillStyle = pct >= 90 ? '#FFA500' : (pct >= 75 ? '#999' : (pct >= 60 ? '#CD7F32' : '#6CA6D9')); const rx = W/2 - tw/2 - 20, ry = 465; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.roundRect(rx, ry, tw + 40, 38, 19); ctx.fill(); ctx.fillStyle = pct >= 90 ? '#5a3800' : '#fff'; ctx.fillText(badgeText, W/2, 490); // Module title ctx.fillStyle = '#2D2A26'; ctx.font = '700 36px "Fraunces", Georgia, serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; const titleText = 'Module ' + moduleId + ' · ' + m.title; // Word wrap if needed if (ctx.measureText(titleText).width > W - 120) { ctx.font = '700 28px "Fraunces", Georgia, serif'; } ctx.fillText(titleText, W/2, 570); // Subtitle ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.5)'; ctx.font = '500 20px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; const subText = m.subtitle.length > 80 ? m.subtitle.substring(0, 77) + '...' : m.subtitle; ctx.fillText(subText, W/2, 610); // Stats boxes const stats = [ { val: String(score), label: 'POINTS' }, { val: pct + '%', label: 'ACCURACY' }, { val: String(streak), label: 'BEST STREAK' } ]; const boxW = 200, boxH = 100, boxGap = 30, startX = W/2 - (boxW * 3 + boxGap * 2) / 2; stats.forEach((s, idx) => { const bx = startX + idx * (boxW + boxGap), by = 660; ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.65)'; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.roundRect(bx, by, boxW, boxH, 16); ctx.fill(); ctx.fillStyle = '#2D2A26'; ctx.font = '800 38px "Fraunces", Georgia, serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; ctx.fillText(s.val, bx + boxW/2, by + 48); ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.45)'; ctx.font = '700 14px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; ctx.fillText(s.label, bx + boxW/2, by + 78); }); // Divider line ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.08)'; ctx.lineWidth = 1; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.moveTo(140, 810); ctx.lineTo(W-140, 810); ctx.stroke(); // Branding ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.35)'; ctx.font = '600 20px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; ctx.textAlign = 'center'; ctx.fillText('Indian Knowledge Systems · Crash Course', W/2, 870); // Watermark ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.25)'; ctx.font = '500 18px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; ctx.fillText('Crafted by Harsha Vardhan · harshabiyyapu.one', W/2, 920); // URL ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.18)'; ctx.font = '400 16px "Plus Jakarta Sans", sans-serif'; ctx.fillText('linkedin.com/in/harsha-biyyapu', W/2, 955); // Star decorations ctx.font = '28px serif'; ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.07)'; const starPositions = [[80,150],[950,200],[120,700],[900,680],[200,900],[850,880],[500,140]]; starPositions.forEach(([sx,sy]) => ctx.fillText('✦', sx, sy)); // Convert to blob and share try { const blob = await new Promise(r => c.toBlob(r, 'image/png')); const file = new File([blob], `iks-module-${moduleId}-${grade}.png`, { type: 'image/png' }); if (navigator.share && navigator.canShare && navigator.canShare({ files: [file] })) { await navigator.share({ title: `IKS Module ${moduleId} — Grade ${grade}`, text: `I scored ${grade} (${pct}% accuracy) on Module ${moduleId}: ${m.title} in the IKS Crash Course! ${badge}`, files: [file] }); } else { // Fallback: download the image const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob); const a = document.createElement('a'); a.href = url; a.download = `iks-module-${moduleId}-${grade}.png`; document.body.appendChild(a); a.click(); document.body.removeChild(a); URL.revokeObjectURL(url); toast('Card image downloaded! Share it on social media 🎉'); } } catch(e) { // Fallback: copy text const text = `${badge} IKS Module ${moduleId}: ${m.title}\nGrade: ${grade} · Accuracy: ${pct}% · Score: ${score} pts\n\n#IKS #IIMBangalore #IndianKnowledgeSystems`; try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text); toast('Share text copied to clipboard!'); } catch(e2) { toast('Could not share — try screenshotting the card!'); } } } // ===== RESET ===== function resetModule(moduleId) { const m = moduleMeta[moduleId]; if (!m) return; if (!confirm('Reset Module ' + moduleId + ' (' + m.title + ')? This will erase your grade, score, and attempt history for this module only.')) return; delete state.moduleProgress[moduleId]; saveProgress(); toast('Module ' + moduleId + ' reset. Fresh start!'); renderHome(); } function resetAllProgress() { if (!confirm('This will erase all your scores, grades, and attempt history for every module. Are you sure?')) return; state.moduleProgress = {}; try { localStorage.removeItem(PROGRESS_KEY); } catch(e) {} toast('Progress reset. Fresh start!'); renderHome(); } // ===== CHEATSHEET VIEW ===== function showCheatsheet() { $('screen').style.display = 'none'; $('cheatsheetView').classList.add('show'); $('backBtn').classList.add('show'); $('brandText').textContent = 'IKS · Visual Cheatsheet'; $('scorePill').classList.remove('show'); $('streakPill').classList.remove('show'); $('progressTrack').classList.remove('show'); $('moduleTracker').classList.remove('show'); window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }); } function hideCheatsheet() { $('cheatsheetView').classList.remove('show'); $('screen').style.display = ''; } function cheatJump(id) { const el = document.getElementById(id); if (el) el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' }); } // ===== INIT ===== loadProgress(); renderHome();
